mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in monthly retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 55% of months when RSAFS is above its trailing 12-month average, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) larger than 1% indicates weakening economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in a contraction.
failure_condition: If after such a decline the S&P 500 posts a positive 20-day return more than 40% of the time, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below the year-over-year CPI inflation rate, real interest rates are negative, which reduces the discount rate on equities and supports higher stock prices over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10) during periods of negative real rates, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment environment (UNRATE above 8%), a decline in the unemployment rate by at least 0.2 percentage points in a month signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts investor confidence and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is negative in more than half of the months when UNRATE falls by 0.2pp or more while UNRATE is above 8%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate profits and drives S&P 500 prices upward over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of GDPC1 quarterly growth vs 60-day forward returns is less than 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 0.50%, monetary policy is extremely accommodative, creating a favorable environment for equities as the opportunity cost of holding stocks is low, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day return is negative in more than 40% of the weeks when FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.50%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in nonfarm payrolls by more than 150,000 and a decline in the unemployment rate by at least 0.1 percentage point in a month indicates a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer spending and drives short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 150K AND UNRATE monthly change ≤ -0.1
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 45% of instances where both conditions hold, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis environment where housing starts are at very low levels (below 600K), a recovery in housing starts above that threshold signals a turning point in the housing market and overall economy, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day return is negative in more than 40% of the quarters following a cross above 600K, the hypothesis fails.
